[[Fraser Anderson]] (brother) }} }} '''Louis Manu "Lui" Anderson''' (born 27 June 1985) is a New Zealand [[rugby league]] footballer who plays for [[Villegailhenc-Aragon XIII]] in the [[Elite Two Championship]]. A New Zealand former international representative forward, he previously played for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in the [[National Rugby League]] competition and for the [[Catalans Dragons]] and [[Warrington Wolves]] in the Super League. He is the brother of [[Vinnie Anderson|Vinnie]] and [[Fraser Anderson]], and is a member of [[the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints]]. Anderson played for Warrington in their [[Challenge Cup 2010#Final|2010 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]].{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8938067.stm|title=Leeds 6-30 Warrington|publisher=BBC|date=28 August 2010|access-date=22 November 2019|first=Ged|last=Scott}} In 2011, he agreed a 3-year-deal with [[Catalans Dragons]]. ==Background== Anderson was born in [[Dargaville]], New Zealand. ==Playing career== His junior clubs were the [[East Coast Bays Barracudas]] in Auckland and the [[Taniwharau Rugby League Club]] in Huntly. He made his first grade début for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] against the [[Penrith Panthers]] at [[Ericsson Stadium Auckland|Ericsson Stadium]] on 28 March 2004. Anderson represented the [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] national side and played for the [[Junior Kiwis]] in 2002 and Captained them in 2003. Anderson was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Tonga|Tonga]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]] but withdrew due to injury.{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7658506.stm|title=Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads|publisher=BBC|date=8 October 2008|access-date=2008-10-09}} He played in the [[2009 Challenge Cup Final ]] victory over Huddersfield and went back to back in the [[2010 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |location=London |work=The Guardian |title=Chris Hicks hat-trick leads Warrington to Challenge Cup triumph over Leeds |first=Andy |last=Wilson |date=28 August 2010 |access-date=22 November 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100831105934/http://www.guardian.co.uk/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |archive-date=31 August 2010 |url-status=live}} Anderson was selected for the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]] squad for the [[International Origin]] match against [[England national rugby league team|England]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]] on 10 June 2011.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/13278410|title=Brian McClennan completes Exiles squad to face England fixture|work=BBC Sport|date=4 May 2011|archive-date=13 January 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160113141331/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/13278410|url-status=live}} ==Legacy== In 2015, he was named in the {{rlp|sr}} in [[Taniwharau Rugby League Club|Taniwharau's]] team of their first 70 years.{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/65740747/lance-hohaia-named-taniwharau-rugby-league-clubs-best|title=Lance Hohaia named Taniwharau rugby league club's best|author=AARON GOILE|date=3 February 2015|work=Stuff}} == Match Fit == Anderson made his debut for "Match Fit: Union vs. League" in 2024. He made his debut with [[Paul Whatuira]] and [[Carlos Spencer]] in Match Fit: Union vs. League. Despite having a lower metabolic age than his biological age, it was revealed that both he and Spencer have a family history of high blood cholesterol despite having lower metabolic age than biological age and are two of the fittest in the group. He also lacks the enzyme to effectively break down cholesterol. Anderson finished second on the initial bronco test at 5:39, two seconds behind Paul Whatuira.{{Cite web |title=Match Fit Season 4 Ep 2 {{!}} SPORT {{!}} ThreeNow |url=https://www.threenow.co.nz/shows/match-fit/season-4-ep-2/S2339-580/M87690-532 |access-date=2024-08-01 |website=www.threenow.co.nz}} Despite being a good [[Butterfly stroke|butterfly]] and [[Freestyle swimming|freestyle]] swimmer, he suffered [[Post-traumatic stress disorder|PTSD]], panic attacks depression and suicidal thoughts after he and his two teenage nieces almost drowned in France after being caught in a rip.{{Cite web |title=Match Fit Season 4 Ep 4 {{!}} SPORT {{!}} ThreeNow |url=https://www.threenow.co.nz/shows/match-fit/season-4-ep-4/S2339-580/M88568-112 |access-date=2024-08-14 |website=www.threenow.co.nz}} At the final weigh-in, while he didn't change in terms of weight, he gained 4kg of muscle while losing 4kg of fat, which meant his metabolic age became the youngest overall at 23, which is a perfect -15 off his biological age.{{Cite web |title=Match Fit Season 4 Ep 6 {{!}} SPORT {{!}} ThreeNow |url=https://www.threenow.co.nz/shows/match-fit/season-4-ep-6/S2339-580/M89103-687 |access-date=2024-08-28 |website=www.threenow.co.nz}} In the final match, both brothers scored a try. Louis scored the opening try under [[rugby league]] rules, while Vinnie scored under [[rugby union]] rules. ==References== {{Reflist|30em}} ==External links== *[http://www.catalansdragons.com/en/fiche-15-anderson-louis/ Catalans Dragons profile] *[http://www.warringtonwolves.org/PlayerDisplay.ink?skip=1&season=2008&Playertype=s Profile at warringtonwolves.com]{{dead link|date=January 2025|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}} *[http://www.rugby-league.com/superleague/player-info/2027 Statistics at rugby-league.com] {{Warrington Wolves - 2009 Challenge Cup Final winners}} {{Warrington Wolves - 2010 Challenge Cup Final winners}} {{New Zealand 2004 Rugby League Tri Nations squad}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Anderson, Louis}} [[Category:1985 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:21st-century New Zealand sportsmen]] [[Category:Anderson brothers|Louis Anderson]] [[Category:Auckland rugby league team players]] [[Category:Catalans Dragons players]] [[Category:East Coast Bays Barracudas players]] [[Category:Exiles rugby league team players]] [[Category:New Zealand Latter Day Saints]] [[Category:New Zealand Māori rugby league players]] [[Category:New Zealand national rugby league team players]] [[Category:New Zealand people of Tongan descent]] [[Category:Sportspeople of Tongan descent]] [[Category:New Zealand rugby league players]] [[Category:New Zealand Warriors players]] [[Category:North Harbour rugby league team players]] [[Category:People educated at the Church College of New Zealand]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Dargaville]] [[Category:Rugby league second-rows]] [[Category:Taniwharau Rugby League Club players]] [[Category:Villegailhenc Aragon XIII players]] [[Category:Warrington Wolves players]]
